formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Delaying the pour of addition silicones improves cast quality by allowing sufficient time for hydrogen gas release, reducing pitting risk associated with incomplete polymerization. Clinical guidelines support a minimum wait period of 30 minutes before pouring to enhance accuracy and integrity of the resulting cast.  
\boxed{D}

The provided summary contains multiple instances of "based on" statements which may reflect redundancy rather than concise synthesis. A more focused version would eliminate repetitive phrasing while preserving clarity about the core mechanism linking delay to improved outcomes.

Corrected Summary:

Delays in pouring addition silicones help minimize pitting from retained hydrogen gas during curing, leading to higher-quality casts. Therefore, postponing the pour by at least 30 minutes is recommended to improve cast performance. 
\boxed{D}. Answer choice: D
